description: We provide independent expert advice to the UK government to create better regulation, reduce unnecessary burdens on business and support economic growth. RPC is an advisory non-departmental public body, sponsored by the Department for Business and Trade .

details.body:
We provide independent expert advice to the UK government to create better regulation, reduce unnecessary burdens on business and support economic growth.

RPC is an advisory non-departmental public body, sponsored by the Department for Business and Trade.

ordered_featured_documents.summary: Independent scrutiny of the evidence and analysis supporting the case for why regulation is necessary, listed by government department.
ordered_featured_documents.title: Regulatory Policy Committee opinions

ordered_featured_documents.summary: Documents and resources to help you apply the Better Regulation Framework when developing and evaluating policies and legislation that regulate business.
ordered_featured_documents.title: Using the Better Regulation Framework

ordered_featured_documents.summary: Guidance to help government officials when submitting cases for independent scrutiny to the Regulatory Policy Committee under the Better Regulation Framework.
ordered_featured_documents.title: RPC guidance for departments and regulators

details.body:
The Chair is responsible for:

  • leading a committee of members to provide independent expert advice to ministers on whether the impact assessments, options assessments and post-implementation reviews for regulations produced by government departments are fit for purpose
  • building relationships across government and engaging with parliamentarians, businesses, civil society, academics and the public
  • contributing to the design and development of the wider regulatory landscape in the UK
  • engaging internationally with other regulatory bodies to exchange evidence and good practice

role_appointments.title: Stephen Gibson - Acting Chair, Regulatory Policy Committee

details.body:
Regulatory Policy Committee members are responsible for:

  • scrutinising the quality of the evidence and analysis supporting the case for why government regulation is necessary under the Better Regulation Framework
  • providing independent expert advice on whether the impact assessments, options assessments and post-implementation reviews for regulations produced by government departments are fit for purpose

Regulatory Policy Committee members are responsible for:

  • scrutinising the quality of the evidence and analysis supporting the case for why government regulation is necessary under the Better Regulation Framework
  • providing independent expert advice on whether the impact assessments, options assessments and post-implementation reviews for regulations produced by government departments are fit for purpose

The Regulatory Policy Committee consists of 8 independent experts with experience and current knowledge of business, employee, consumer and economics issues. Members were appointed after an open competition which followed the Commissioner for Public Appointments’ code of practice.

  • providing independent expert advice on whether the impact assessments, options assessments and post-implementation reviews for regulations produced by government departments are fit for purpose
